MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPP OF OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S DEMURRER; POS FILED BY PLAINTIFF UPADHYAY, VANDANA
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-31-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS THE FARISSE LAW FIRM, SIMONA FARISSE AND CARLA MINNARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BY JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS FARISSE LAW FIRM, SIMONA FARISSE AND CARLA MINNARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CLAIM IS TIME-BARRED. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DEMONSTRATE PLAINTIFF KNEW, ON MAY 3, 2010, SHE RELEASED ALL HER RIGHTS TO ANY EMPLOYEE BENEFIT PLANS PURSUANT TO THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PERIOD WAS, THEREFORE, TRIGGERED ON MAY 3, 2010 AND EXPIRED ON MAY 3, 2011. THE ACTUAL INJURY SUFFERED BY PLAINTIFF WAS THE LOSS OF BENEFITS WHICH OCCURRED UPON EXECUTION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ON MAY 3, 2010 THUS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS TIME WAS NOT TOLLED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976 =

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2012 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2012 Continued To Sep-19-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GEORGE E. CLAUSE OF HAYES SCOTT BONINO & MCLAY, LLP, TEL. NO. (650) 486-2874 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL W. HAGER OF ROECA HAAS HAGER LLP, TEL. NO. 352-0980 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS THE FARISSE LAW FIRM, SIMONA FARISSE AND CARLA MINNARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BY JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW.
AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS FARISSE LAW FIRM, SIMONA FARISSE AND CARLA MINNARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. The claim is time-barred. The allegations in the First Amended Complaint demonstrate Plaintiff knew, on May 3, 2010, she released all her rights to any employee benefit plans pursuant to the settlement agreement. The statute of limitations period was, therefore, triggered on May 3, 2010 and expired on May 3, 2011. The actual injury suffered by Plaintiff was the loss of benefits which occurred upon execution of the settlement agreement on May 3, 2010 thus the statute of limitations time was not tolled. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/CK

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT FARISSE LAW FIRM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar. A First Amended Complaint was filed May 30, 2012. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
